text: In "The Miracle of Mindfulness," Thich Nhat Hanh describes washing dishes as a complete meditative act — not a chore to be finished so that tea can begin, but a moment deserving full presence in itself. If you wash dishes while thinking of tea, he warns, you are equally incapable of drinking tea mindfully; you will think of the next thing. This is the structure of distraction: each moment treated as a corridor to somewhere else. Dishwashing, precisely because it is humble, serves as the ideal test of whether we can inhabit experience directly rather than perpetually deferring arrival.
question: According to Thich Nhat Hanh, what happens if you wash dishes while thinking about tea?

options.text: You will enjoy the tea more after completing the task
You will be equally incapable of being present when drinking tea
You develop a habit of treating chores as meditation
You miss the spiritual lesson embedded in routine work
